transcript.content: Create a basic Doodle-clone. Users must be able to sign up and log in using email+password (no account creation confirmation link needed), create events (title, description) and propose times. Then they can share a link to the event. Otzher users can open the link and vote for their preferred times. The creator then sees the votes (not other users). At anypoint the creator can close the voting and select a time slot. Other users see the selected time slot.

Creating complete Doodle-clone backend with 4 collections, 6 mutations, 3 queries, and proper authentication handling for creators vs public voters.

Your input fields are:
1. `conversation_log` (str): The raw conversation log or requirements text.
Your output fields are:
1. `output` (TestScriptOutput): The structured test script.
All interactions will be structured in the following way, with the appropriate values filled in.

[[ ## conversation_log ## ]]
{conversation_log}

[[ ## output ## ]]
{output}        # note: the value you produce must adhere to the JSON schema: {"type": "object", "$defs": {"UXTask": {"type": "object", "properties": {"acceptance_criteria": {"type": "string", "description": "The specific visible outcome that confirms success.", "title": "Acceptance Criteria"}, "number": {"type": "integer", "description": "The sequence number of the task, starting from 1.", "title": "Number"}, "requirement": {"type": "string", "description": "The high-level requirement. MUST specify the Actor (e.g., 'User 1 (Host) must...').", "title": "Requirement"}, "steps": {"type": "array", "description": "A list of STRICTLY ATOMIC actions. Must start with verbs like: 'Locate', 'Click', 'Type', 'Verify', 'Wait', 'Scan', 'Switch', or 'Open'.", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "Steps"}}, "required": ["number", "requirement", "steps", "acceptance_criteria"], "title": "UXTask"}}, "properties": {"reasoning": {"type": "string", "description": "Step-back analysis: 1. Map Resource Lifecycle (Create -> Join). 2. Identify Negative Tests (errors/disabled states). 3. Plan Tab switching.", "title": "Reasoning"}, "ux_tasks": {"type": "array", "description": "The linear, executable list of UX tasks.", "items": {"$ref": "#/$defs/UXTask"}, "title": "Ux Tasks"}}, "required": ["reasoning", "ux_tasks"], "title": "TestScriptOutput"}

[[ ## completed ## ]]
In adhering to this structure, your objective is: 
        Translate a conversation log into a linear, executable QA test script for browser automation.
        
        CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS:
        1. ATOMICITY: Steps must be single actions. Never combine actions (e.g., "Login and Click" is FORBIDDEN).
           - BAD: "Navigate to URL and login"
           - GOOD: "Open new tab", "Navigate to URL", "Type 'user'", "Click 'Login'"
        2. NEGATIVE TESTING: Verify error states or disabled buttons BEFORE performing the successful action.
        3. TAB MANAGEMENT: Explicitly manage state. Use "Switch to tab 1" or "Open new tab for User 2".
        4. LIFECYCLE: A resource must be CREATED (User 1) before it can be JOINED (User 2).
        5. IMPLICIT EDGE CASES: Beyond explicit requirements, infer what SHOULD fail based on common sense:
           - If there's authentication, test accessing protected resources without logging in.
           - If there are inputs with logical constraints, test invalid/impossible values.
           - If there's authorization (roles/ownership), test actions by unauthorized users.
           - If there's data validation, test boundary violations the app should reject.

[[ ## conversation_log ## ]]

agent1_output.reasoning: The test plan follows the lifecycle of a Doodle-clone event. 1. Authentication: User 1 (Creator) must sign up. A negative test is included for the password requirement error seen in the log. 2. Creation: User 1 creates an event with time slots. 3. Sharing/Voting: A second tab is opened for User 2 (Voter) to join via a shareable link. User 2 votes. 4. Authorization: User 2 is checked to ensure they cannot see results or close the poll. 5. Finalization: User 1 closes the poll and selects a winner, which is then verified on User 2's screen.

ux_tasks.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must see an error when attempting to sign up with an invalid password.
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: The application displays an error message 'Invalid password' or similar validation message.

ux_tasks.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must be able to sign up and log in successfully.
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: User 1 is redirected to the dashboard or home screen after successful authentication.

ux_tasks.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must create a new event with proposed time slots.
steps: Type 'Weekly sync up lunch' into 'Description' field
Type '2023-12-01T12:00' into the time slot input
Type '2023-12-02T13:00' into the second time slot input
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: The event is created and a shareable link/URL is displayed to the user.

ux_tasks.requirement: User 2 (Voter) must be able to vote on the event via the shareable link.
steps: Locate the shareable link on User 1's screen
Navigate to the shareable link URL
Verify 'Close Event' button is NOT visible to User 2
Verify vote counts are NOT visible to User 2
Select the first time slot checkbox
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: User 2 sees a confirmation that their vote was recorded.

ux_tasks.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must see the vote counts and be able to close the voting with a selected time.
steps: Wait for the event page to refresh
Verify the first time slot shows '1 vote'
Select the first time slot as the final choice
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: The event status changes to 'Closed' and the selected time is highlighted.

ux_tasks.requirement: User 2 (Voter) must see the final selected time slot.
steps: Wait for the page to refresh
Verify the text 'Event Closed' is visible
Verify the selected time slot (2023-12-01 12:00) is displayed as the winner
ux_tasks.acceptance_criteria: The voter sees the final decision made by the creator.

task_results.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must see an error when attempting to sign up with an invalid password.
task_results.observations: Task passed. Validation error 'Password must be at least 8 characters' was displayed correctly as a notification toast and below the password field.

task_results.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must be able to sign up and log in successfully.
task_results.observations: Task passed. Successful sign-up and automatic login occurred, redirecting User 1 to the dashboard with a 'Account created successfully!' toast.

task_results.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must create a new event with proposed time slots.
task_results.observations: Task passed. Event 'Team Lunch' was created successfully with two time slots. Required scrolling down to click the final 'Create Event' button (minor UX friction).

task_results.requirement: User 2 (Voter) must be able to vote on the event via the shareable link.
task_results.observations: CRITICAL FAILURE: Time slots added by User 1 were not visible on the voting page (Tab 6E0E), displaying the message: 'Time slot selection will be available once the event organizer adds time options.' After inputting voter details, clicking 'Submit Vote' resulted in the button getting stuck in a 'Submitting...' state (logic error).
task_results.advice: Investigate why time slots are not being loaded/rendered on the voting page for participants. Ensure the vote submission API handles missing time slot selections gracefully or prevents submission without selection.

task_results.requirement: User 1 (Creator) must see the vote counts and be able to close the voting with a selected time.
task_results.observations: CRITICAL FAILURE: After clicking 'Close Voting', the UI failed to update to the post-closure state. The page remained stuck, displaying the pre-voting message ('Event management features will be available once participants start voting.') and the 'Close Voting' button. Navigating away and back did not resolve the rendering issue, preventing the creator from selecting the final time slot.
task_results.advice: Investigate the state management and rendering logic triggered by the 'Close Voting' action. The UI must transition to the final time slot selection view immediately upon closure, regardless of whether votes were cast.

task_results.summary: The sign-up and event creation flows are functional. However, the core voting and event finalization flows are critically broken. Task 4 failed because time slots were not visible to voters, and Task 5 failed because the creator could not select a final time after closing the voting due to a UI rendering failure.

grouped_tests.test_name: The system must validate password complexity during account creation
grouped_tests.description: Validates that the application prevents account creation with weak passwords and provides immediate feedback. This ensures security standards are enforced at the point of entry.
grouped_tests.summary: Password validation correctly identified and blocked an invalid password attempt.

password_validation_error.observation: Validation error 'Password must be at least 8 characters' was displayed correctly as a notification toast and below the password field.

grouped_tests.test_name: The user must be able to successfully create an account and access the dashboard
grouped_tests.description: Verifies the end-to-end registration flow with valid credentials. Successful completion is critical for user onboarding and access to core features.
grouped_tests.summary: User successfully registered and was automatically redirected to the authenticated dashboard.

account_registration.observation: Successful sign-up and automatic login occurred, redirecting User 1 to the dashboard with a 'Account created successfully!' toast.

grouped_tests.test_name: The user must be able to create a new event with multiple time slot options
grouped_tests.description: Validates the event creation workflow, including title, description, and the addition of time slots. This is the primary functional requirement for the event organizer.
grouped_tests.summary: The event was successfully created with two time slots, though minor UI friction was noted regarding button placement.

event_creation_flow.observation: Event 'Team Lunch' was created successfully with two time slots. Required scrolling down to click the final 'Create Event' button.

grouped_tests.test_name: The system must allow participants to view time slots and submit votes
grouped_tests.description: Tests the voter-facing side of the application, ensuring that shared links correctly display the event data and allow for interaction. This is critical for the collaborative aspect of the tool.
grouped_tests.summary: CRITICAL FAILURE: Time slots failed to render for the voter, and the submission process became unresponsive.

voter_interface_and_submission.observation: Time slots added by User 1 were not visible on the voting page; clicking 'Submit Vote' resulted in the button getting stuck in a 'Submitting...' state.
voter_interface_and_submission.advice: Investigate why time slots are not being loaded/rendered on the voting page for participants. Ensure the vote submission API handles missing time slot selections gracefully.

grouped_tests.test_name: The application must allow the creator to close voting and finalize a time slot
grouped_tests.description: Validates the administrative flow of ending a poll and selecting a winning time slot. This transition is necessary to move the event from the 'Active' to 'Closed' state.
grouped_tests.summary: CRITICAL FAILURE: The UI failed to transition to the selection state after the 'Close Voting' action was triggered.

close_voting_and_selection.observation: After clicking 'Close Voting', the UI failed to update to the post-closure state. The page remained stuck on the pre-voting message.
close_voting_and_selection.advice: Investigate the state management and rendering logic triggered by the 'Close Voting' action. The UI must transition to the final time slot selection view immediately.
